Ahmedabad.title: Ahmedabad Heat Action Plan
Ahmedabad.source: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation / Natural Resources Defense Council
Ahmedabad.summary: India's first city-level heat action plan, developed after the 2010 heat wave killed over 1,300 people in Ahmedabad in a single week.

Ahmedabad.title: Effect of Heat Waves on Mortality in Ahmedabad, India
Ahmedabad.summary: Peer-reviewed study documenting excess mortality during Ahmedabad's 2010 heat wave, with daily temperatures exceeding 46°C; established the epidemiological basis for the city's heat response system.

Bangalore.title: Bengaluru's Water Crisis: Causes, Impacts and the Way Forward
Bangalore.summary: Analysis of the 2023 Bengaluru water crisis driven by below-normal northeast monsoon, depleted Cauvery allocations, and rapid urban expansion outpacing water infrastructure.

Bangalore.title: Bengaluru Faces Acute Water Shortage as Reservoirs Hit Record Lows
Bangalore.summary: Reports on Cauvery reservoir levels falling to historic lows in early 2024, forcing BWSSB to cut daily supply by 40% and deepening the city's dependence on tanker water.

Chennai.title: Chennai's Day Zero: How India's Sixth-Largest City Ran Out of Water
Chennai.summary: Covers the June 2019 crisis when Chennai's four main reservoirs ran dry simultaneously due to consecutive weak northeast monsoon seasons, forcing the city to rely entirely on groundwater and tankers.

Chennai.title: North-East Monsoon 2023 Season Report – Chennai
Chennai.summary: IMD seasonal report documenting high variability in northeast monsoon rainfall over Tamil Nadu, with intense short-duration events replacing steady rainfall, increasing urban flood and dry-spell risk.

Delhi.title: Delhi Records Hottest Day in History at 49.2°C
Delhi.summary: IMD data confirming Delhi's Mungeshpur station recorded 49.2°C on 29 May 2024, the highest temperature ever logged in the capital, with the heat wave persisting for 12 consecutive days.

Delhi.title: Heat Wave Conditions in India – Situation Report
Delhi.summary: NDMA situation report on the 2022 pre-monsoon heat wave across Northwest India, noting Delhi and Haryana wheat yields fell 10–35% due to a heat-driven premature ripening event.

Hyderabad.title: Ground Water Year Book – Telangana State
Hyderabad.source: Central Ground Water Board (CGWB), Ministry of Jal Shakti
Hyderabad.summary: Annual CGWB assessment showing over 60% of Hyderabad's peri-urban mandals with declining groundwater trends, attributed to erratic southwest monsoon and unregulated borewell extraction.

Hyderabad.title: Hyderabad Rains: Uneven Distribution Worsens Flood-Drought Cycle
Hyderabad.summary: Documents how Hyderabad's rainfall has become spatially concentrated, with some zones receiving 200% of normal while others receive less than 50%, stressing both drainage and agricultural water supply simultaneously.

Jaipur.title: Ground Water Year Book – Rajasthan
Jaipur.source: Central Ground Water Board (CGWB), Ministry of Jal Shakti
Jaipur.summary: CGWB report classifying over 90% of Rajasthan's blocks as 'over-exploited' or 'critical', with Jaipur district groundwater levels declining at 0.5–1 m per year in irrigated zones.

Jaipur.title: Rajasthan Drought Assessment and Crop Loss Report 2018–19
Jaipur.summary: State government assessment recording 44 of 33 Rajasthan districts as drought-affected in 2018–19, with Jaipur region kharif and rabi crop losses exceeding 30% due to rainfall deficit.

Kolkata.title: Cyclone Amphan – Situation Report No. 5
Kolkata.summary: Official NDMA report on Cyclone Amphan (May 2020), which made landfall near Kolkata as a super cyclonic storm, causing widespread flooding, infrastructure damage to 2.4 million homes, and disruption to kharif sowing.

Kolkata.title: West Bengal Flood Report – Monsoon Season
Kolkata.source: West Bengal State Disaster Management Authority (WBSDMA)
Kolkata.summary: WBSDMA report documenting recurrent monsoon flooding in Kolkata's low-lying wards (Topsia, Tiljala, Garden Reach) caused by intensified rainfall events and high tidal backwater from the Hooghly River.

Lucknow.title: Ground Water Year Book – Uttar Pradesh
Lucknow.source: Central Ground Water Board (CGWB), Ministry of Jal Shakti
Lucknow.summary: CGWB annual report showing significant groundwater depletion in Lucknow and surrounding districts driven by rice-wheat double-cropping irrigation demand and below-normal monsoon recharge.

Lucknow.title: Crop Water Stress and Yield Loss in Uttar Pradesh – Kharif 2022
Lucknow.source: Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare / FASAL Project
Lucknow.summary: FASAL remote-sensing crop assessment indicating Lucknow and Hardoi districts experienced erratic mid-season monsoon breaks in 2022, reducing paddy yields by an estimated 15–20% versus the 5-year average.

Mumbai.title: Mumbai Floods July 2005 – Post-Event Analysis
Mumbai.source: IMD Pune / Municipal Corporation of Greater Mumbai (MCGM)
Mumbai.summary: Post-event analysis of the July 2005 Mumbai flood where 944 mm of rain fell in 24 hours at Santacruz, killing over 1,000 people and causing economic losses exceeding ₹550 crore.

Mumbai.title: Extreme Rainfall Events in Mumbai: Trends and Projections
Mumbai.source: Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM), Pune
Mumbai.summary: IITM study showing a statistically significant increase in very heavy rainfall days (>115 mm/day) over Mumbai during 1951–2019, projecting further intensification under RCP 4.5 and 8.5 scenarios.

Pune.title: Maharashtra Drought 2018–19: District-Level Crop Loss Assessment
Pune.summary: State government assessment declaring 26 of 36 Maharashtra districts drought-affected in 2018–19, with Pune district reporting significant sugarcane and onion losses due to a 30–40% rainfall deficit.

Pune.title: Variability of Southwest Monsoon Rainfall Over Pune District
Pune.source: Journal of Earth System Science / IMD
Pune.summary: Analysis of 100 years of IMD rain-gauge data at Pune showing increasing inter-annual variability, with a trend toward more intense but shorter rain spells that reduce effective soil moisture recharge for rainfed crops.
